title: haplopappus taeda
intro: Haplopappus taeda, also known as the golden everlasting, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Haplopappus taeda include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as supporting the immune system. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, as well as in topical creams and ointments.
